chapter_context.summary: Solomon disregards God's command and marries many foreign wives who turn his heart to idolatry; God raises adversaries (Hadad, Rezon, Jeroboam) and decrees the kingdom's division; Solomon dies.

outline.text: Solomon's Many Foreign Wives Turn His Heart to Idolatry
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Many Foreign Wives Turn His Heart to Idolatry (11:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: God's Anger; Proclaims the Tearing Away of the Kingdom
outline.explanation: This section focuses on God's Anger; Proclaims the Tearing Away of the Kingdom (11:9-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Solomon's Adversaries: Hadad the Edomite and Rezon of Damascus
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Solomon's Adversaries: Hadad the Edomite and Rezon of Damascus (11:14-25), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Jeroboam's Rebellion; Ahijah's Prophecy of the Ten Tribes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jeroboam's Rebellion; Ahijah's Prophecy of the Ten Tribes (11:26-40), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: The Death and Burial of Solomon; Rehoboam Succeeds Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Death and Burial of Solomon; Rehoboam Succeeds Him (11:41-43), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 11, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
